chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا وَ لَمْ يُدْخَلْ بِهَا وَ مَا لَهَا مِنَ الصَّدَاقِ وَ الْعِدَّةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – The woman widowed from her husband and not having been slept with, and what is for her from the dower, and the waiting period

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/203)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/203)
text: إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ قَدْ دَخَلَ بِهَا وَ قَدْ فَرَضَ لَهَا مَهْراً فَلَهَا نِصْفُ مَا فَرَضَ لَهَا وَ لَهَا الْمِيرَاثُ وَ عَلَيْهَا الْعِدَّةُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If she did not happen to have been slept with, and the dower was obligated for her, so for her is half of what had been obligated for her, and for her is the inheritance, and upon her is the waiting period’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one has not gone to bed with his wife but has set up mahr (dower), she deserves one-half of the mahr (dower) that is set for her and complete inheritance. She must count complete waiting period.’”
